inputs.name: cds_jwt_iss
inputs.label: CRD JWT Issuer
inputs.description:
The `iss` claim of the JWT in the Authorization header sent by the CRD client under test on
              all CRD requests. This value will be used to associate incoming requests with this test
              session and any requests that use a different `iss` value will not be recognized.

inputs.name: cds_jwk_set
inputs.label: CRD JSON Web Key Set (JWKS)
inputs.description:
The CRD client's JWK Set containing it's public key. May be either
              a publicly accessible url containing the JWKS, or the raw JWKS.
              This input is required for these tests to pass.

inputs.name: cds_jwt_iss
inputs.label: CRD JWT Issuer
inputs.description:
Value of the `iss` claim that must be sent on the Bearer token in the `Authorization`
            header of all requests. Run or re-run the **Client Registration** group to set or
            change this value.

inputs.name: cds_jwk_set
inputs.label: CRD JSON Web Key Set (JWKS)
inputs.description:
The client's registered JWK Set containing it's public key, either
            as a publicly accessible url containing the JWKS, or the raw JWKS.
            Run or re-run the **Client Registration** group to set or
            change this value. Used if the `jku` header is not found in the auth token jwt.

inputs.name: order_select_selected_response_types
inputs.label: Response types to return from order-select hook requests
inputs.description:
Select the cards/action response types that the Inferno hook request endpoints will return. The default
              response type that will be returned for this hook is the `Instructions` card type.

inputs.name: order_select_custom_response_template
inputs.label: Custom response for order-select hook requests
inputs.description:
A JSON string may be provided here to replace the normal response
              from the hook request endpoint

inputs.name: order_select_override_access_token
inputs.label: order-select Prefetch Validation Override Bearer Token
inputs.description:
Optionally provide a bearer token to use in place of the one provided in the hook request
              when checking that the prefetched data is equivalent to what can be access via FHIR.
              Use only if the bearer token in the request will not be valid even right after the hook
              request is made. If provided it must have the same scope as the bearer token provided
              in the hook request.

inputs.name: cds_jwt_iss
inputs.label: CRD JWT Issuer
inputs.description:
Value of the `iss` claim that must be sent on the Bearer token in the `Authorization`
            header of all requests. Run or re-run the **Client Registration** group to set or
            change this value.

inputs.name: cds_jwk_set
inputs.label: CRD JSON Web Key Set (JWKS)
inputs.description:
The client's registered JWK Set containing it's public key, either
            as a publicly accessible url containing the JWKS, or the raw JWKS.
            Run or re-run the **Client Registration** group to set or
            change this value. Used if the `jku` header is not found in the auth token jwt.

inputs.name: order_sign_selected_response_types
inputs.label: Response types to return from order-sign hook requests
inputs.description:
Select the cards/action response types that the Inferno hook request endpoints will return. The default
              response type that will be returned for this hook is the `Coverage Information` card type.

inputs.name: order_sign_custom_response_template
inputs.label: Custom response for order-sign hook requests
inputs.description:
A JSON string may be provided here to replace the normal response
              from the hook request endpoint

inputs.name: order_sign_override_access_token
inputs.label: order-sign Prefetch Validation Override Bearer Token
inputs.description:
Optionally provide a bearer token to use in place of the one provided in the hook request
              when checking that the prefetched data is equivalent to what can be access via FHIR.
              Use only if the bearer token in the request will not be valid even right after the hook
              request is made. If provided it must have the same scope as the bearer token provided
              in the hook request.
